title: Payback Period Rule: If a project has a cost of 100000 and generates...
summary: Calculate the payback period by dividing the initial investment by annual cash inflows.
prompt: If a project has a cost of $100,000 and generates cash flows of $30,000 for 5 years, what is the payback period?
solution: Payback Period = Initial Investment / Annual Cash Flow. Thus, $100,000 / $30,000 = 3.33 years, indicating the payback period is approximately 4 years.
